sigma_rule_title: Cisco Local Accounts
sigma_rule_description: Find local accounts being created or modified as well as remote authentication configurations

sigma_rule_title: ESXi Admin Permission Assigned To Account Via ESXCLI
sigma_rule_description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "system" and "permission" flags in order to assign admin permissions to an account.

sigma_rule_title: Privileged User Has Been Created
sigma_rule_description: Detects the addition of a new user to a privileged group such as "root" or "sudo"

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: /var/log/secure on REHL systems or /var/log/auth.log on debian like Systems needs to be collected in order for this detection to work

sigma_rule_title: Anomalous User Activity
sigma_rule_description: Indicates that there are anomalous patterns of behavior like suspicious changes to the directory.

sigma_rule_title: Bulk Deletion Changes To Privileged Account Permissions
sigma_rule_description: Detects when a user is removed from a privileged role. Bulk changes should be investigated.

sigma_rule_title: Change to Authentication Method
sigma_rule_description: Change to authentication method could be an indicator of an attacker adding an auth method to the account so they can have continued access.

sigma_rule_title: Number Of Resource Creation Or Deployment Activities
sigma_rule_description: Number of VM creations or deployment activities occur in Azure via the azureactivity log.

sigma_rule_title: GCP Access Policy Deleted
sigma_rule_description:
Detects when an access policy that is applied to a GCP cloud resource is deleted.
An adversary would be able to remove access policies to gain access to a GCP cloud resource.

sigma_rule_title: Google Workspace User Granted Admin Privileges
sigma_rule_description: Detects when an Google Workspace user is granted admin privileges.

sigma_rule_title: Google Workspace Granted Domain API Access
sigma_rule_description: Detects when an API access service account is granted domain authority.

sigma_rule_title: AWS User Login Profile Was Modified
sigma_rule_description:
Detects activity when someone is changing passwords on behalf of other users.
An attacker with the "iam:UpdateLoginProfile" permission on other users can change the password used to login to the AWS console on any user that already has a login profile setup.

sigma_rule_title: AWS Route 53 Domain Transferred to Another Account
sigma_rule_description: Detects when a request has been made to transfer a Route 53 domain to another AWS account.

sigma_rule_title: AWS IAM Backdoor Users Keys
sigma_rule_description:
Detects AWS API key creation for a user by another user.
Backdoored users can be used to obtain persistence in the AWS environment.
Also with this alert, you can detect a flow of AWS keys in your org.

sigma_rule_title: AWS Route 53 Domain Transfer Lock Disabled
sigma_rule_description: Detects when a transfer lock was removed from a Route 53 domain. It is recommended to refrain from performing this action unless intending to transfer the domain to a different registrar.

sigma_rule_title: Bitbucket Global Permission Changed
sigma_rule_description: Detects global permissions change activity.

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: "Advance" log level is required to receive these audit events.

sigma_rule_title: DMSA Service Account Created in Specific OUs - PowerShell
sigma_rule_description:
Detects the creation of a dMSA service account using the New-ADServiceAccount cmdlet in certain OUs.
The fact that the cmdlet is used to create a dMSASvc account in a specific OU is highly suspicious.
It is a pattern trying to exploit the BadSuccessor privilege escalation vulnerability in Windows Server 2025.
On top of that, if the user that is creating the dMSASvc account is not a legitimate administrator or does not have the necessary permissions,
it is a strong signal of an attempted or successful abuse of the BaDSuccessor vulnerability for privilege escalation within the Windows Server 2025 Active Directory environment.

sigma_rule_title: Powershell LocalAccount Manipulation
sigma_rule_description:
Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain access to victim systems.
Account manipulation may consist of any action that preserves adversary access to a compromised account, such as modifying credentials or permission groups

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: Script Block Logging must be enabled

sigma_rule_title: DMSA Link Attributes Modified
sigma_rule_description:
Detects modification of dMSA link attributes (msDS-ManagedAccountPrecededByLink) via PowerShell scripts.
This command line pattern could be an indicator an attempt to exploit the BadSuccessor privilege escalation vulnerability in Windows Server 2025.

sigma_rule_title: Password Set to Never Expire via WMI
sigma_rule_description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to modify user account settings and explicitly disable password expiration.

sigma_rule_title: User Added to Local Administrators Group
sigma_rule_description: Detects addition of users to the local administrator group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".
sigma_rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

sigma_rule_title: User Added To Highly Privileged Group
sigma_rule_description: Detects addition of users to highly privileged groups via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".

sigma_rule_title: New DMSA Service Account Created in Specific OUs
sigma_rule_description:
Detects the creation of a dMSASvc account using the New-ADServiceAccount cmdlet in certain OUs.
The fact that the Cmdlet is used to create a dMSASvc account in a specific OU is highly suspicious.
It is a pattern trying to exploit the BadSuccessor privilege escalation vulnerability in Windows Server 2025.
On top of that, if the user that is creating the dMSASvc account is not a legitimate administrator or does not have the necessary permissions,
it is a strong signal of an attempted or successful abuse of the BaDSuccessor vulnerability for privilege escalation within the Windows Server 2025 Active Directory environment.

sigma_rule_title: User Added to Local Administrator Group
sigma_rule_description: Detects the addition of a new member to the local administrator group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity

sigma_rule_title: A New Trust Was Created To A Domain
sigma_rule_description: Addition of domains is seldom and should be verified for legitimacy.

sigma_rule_title: Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right
sigma_rule_description: Backdooring domain object to grant the rights associated with DCSync to a regular user or machine account using Powerview\Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync Extended Right cmdlet, will allow to re-obtain the pwd hashes of any user/computer
sigma_rule_author: Samir Bousseaden, Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, Maxence Fossat

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: The "Audit Directory Service Changes" logging policy must be configured in order to receive events. Audit events are generated only for objects with configured system access control lists (SACLs). Audit events are generated only for objects with configured system access control lists (SACLs) and only when accessed in a manner that matches their SACL settings. This policy covers the following events ids - 5136, 5137, 5138, 5139, 5141. Note that the default policy does not cover User objects. For that a custom AuditRule need to be setup (See https://github.com/OTRF/Set-AuditRule)

sigma_rule_title: Active Directory User Backdoors
sigma_rule_description: Detects scenarios where one can control another users or computers account without having to use their credentials.

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: Audit Policy : Account Management > Audit User Account Management, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\Account Management\Audit User Account Management, DS Access > Audit Directory Service Changes, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\DS Access\Audit Directory Service Changes

sigma_rule_title: Password Change on Directory Service Restore Mode (DSRM) Account
sigma_rule_description:
Detects potential attempts made to set the Directory Services Restore Mode administrator password.
The Directory Service Restore Mode (DSRM) account is a local administrator account on Domain Controllers.
Attackers may change the password in order to obtain persistence.

sigma_rule_title: Enabled User Right in AD to Control User Objects
sigma_rule_description: Detects scenario where if a user is assigned the SeEnableDelegationPrivilege right in Active Directory it would allow control of other AD user objects.

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: Audit Policy : Policy Change > Audit Authorization Policy Change, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\Policy Change\Audit Authorization Policy Change

sigma_rule_title: A Security-Enabled Global Group Was Deleted
sigma_rule_description: Detects activity when a security-enabled global group is deleted

sigma_rule_title: A Member Was Removed From a Security-Enabled Global Group
sigma_rule_description: Detects activity when a member is removed from a security-enabled global group

sigma_rule_title: A Member Was Added to a Security-Enabled Global Group
sigma_rule_description: Detects activity when a member is added to a security-enabled global group

sigma_rule_title: Suspicious Computer Account Name Change CVE-2021-42287
sigma_rule_description: Detects the renaming of an existing computer account to a account name that doesn't contain a $ symbol as seen in attacks against CVE-2021-42287

sigma_rule_title: New MsDS-DelegatedManagedServiceAccount (DMSA) Object Created
sigma_rule_description:
Detects the creation of new msDS-DelegatedManagedServiceAccount objects, which could indicate potential abuse of privilege escalation vulnerabilities in Windows Server 2025.
The msDS-DelegatedManagedServiceAccount (DMSA) is a new object class introduced in Windows Server 2025 that allows administrators to delegate the management of service accounts to other users or groups.
Attackers may exploit this feature to create unauthorized service accounts with elevated privileges, leading to privilege escalation within the Active Directory environment.
It is highly suspicious if an msDS-DelegatedManagedServiceAccount object is created without proper authorization or in an unexpected context, such as by a non-administrative user or outside of normal administrative workflows.
So, it's a good idea to look out for accounts that are not typically responsible for service account creation to detect potential abuse of this feature.

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: Audit Policy : Account Management > Audit User Account Management, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\Account Management\Audit User Account Management, DS Access > Audit Directory Service Changes, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\DS Access\Audit Directory Service Changes

sigma_rule_title: msDS-ManagedAccountPrecededByLink Attribute Modified
sigma_rule_description:
Detects modifications to the msDS-ManagedAccountPrecededByLink attribute, which may indicate an attempted or successful abuse of the BaD-Successor msDS-DelegatedManagedServiceAccount (DMSA) vulnerability.
The DMSA is a new object class introduced in Windows Server 2025 that allows administrators to delegate the management of service accounts to other users or groups.
Changes to this attribute by suspicious accounts or outside of normal administrative workflows are a strong signal of an attempted or successful abuse.
If it is indeed modified by an account that is not typically responsible for such changes, it could indicate an attempt to exploit the BaD-Successor vulnerability for privilege escalation within the Windows Server 2025 Active Directory environment.

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: Audit Policy : Account Management > Audit User Account Management, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\Account Management\Audit User Account Management, DS Access > Audit Directory Service Changes, Group Policy : Computer Configuration\Windows Settings\Security Settings\Advanced Audit Policy Configuration\Audit Policies\DS Access\Audit Directory Service Changes
